photo_observations.decorative_details: Decorative character is primarily expressed through the storefront treatment and surviving upper-storey massing.

facade_description: 240 Augusta Ave presents a bay-and-gable domestic facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, row, bay-and-gable typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a black storefront cladding with orange fascia and signage discs palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 3, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using storefront, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through a one-storey commercial addition.
